He will lengthen out their days] The children of Israel under Moses, like the seed of Lehi, witnessed many miracles in their behalf. God declared: "Therefore shall ye keep all the commandments which I command you this day, that ye may be strong, and go in and possess the land, whither ye go to possess it; and that ye may prolong your days in the land, which the Lord sware unto your fathers to…
Lord thy God, and keep his charge, and his statutes, and - his judgments, and his commandnients, alway.

Israel's climate also plays a key role in God's testing of his people. Israel sits at the southern edge of the European storm belt systems, which means that in some years parts of the land may be untouched by rain. Notice in the following description of the promised land [page 57] given by the Lord how trust in God and obedience to him is serious business for anyone living in his testing ground:…

matches.lb: Halakhah: Contemporary Halakhic Problems, Vol III, Part II, Chapter IX Animal Experimentation 8
matches.x: Judaism posits yet another regulation regarding the welfare of animals which is regarded as biblical in nature even though the law is not reflected in a literal reading of Scripture. The biblical statement "I will give grass in thy fields for thy cattle, and thou shall eat and be satisfied" (Deuteronomy 11:15) is understood in rabbinic exegesis as forbidding a person to partake of any food unless…

And these words-which I command thee this day shall be in thine heart; and thou shalt teach thenv diligently unto thy children, and shalt tali: of them when thou sittest in thine house, and when then walkest by the way, and when thou liest down, and when thou risest up.’1—1)eut. vi., 7, 8.

Beginning with Adam and Eve a pattern was set that parents are to teach their children: "And Adam and Eve blessed the name of God, and they made all things known unto their sons and their daughters" (Moses 5:12). The Lord commanded Moses to "teach [the Lord's words] diligently unto thy children, and … talk of them when thou sittest in thine house, and when thou walkest by the way, and when thou…
